title: Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean

seo.title: How to Make Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean — Easy Korean Recipe

description: Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean is a beloved street food snack, combining a sweet cream cheese filling with a savory garlic butter glaze. This delightful bread offers a perfect balance of flavors and textures, with a crispy exterior and a soft, chewy interior. It's an irresistible treat that's surprisingly easy to make at home, perfect for sharing or enjoying solo.

ingredients.notes: e.g., French boule or brioche bun, about 6-8 inches diameter
optional, for extra sweetness in glaze

instructions.title: Prepare the Bread
instructions.text: Carefully cut the round bread into 6 or 8 wedges, making sure not to cut all the way through the bottom so the pieces remain connected. This creates pockets for the filling and allows for even dipping.

instructions.title: Make the Cream Cheese Filling
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, granulated sugar, and heavy cream. Mix well with a whisk or spatula until the mixture is smooth and creamy.

instructions.title: Fill the Bread
instructions.text: Using a piping bag or a spoon, generously fill the cuts in the bread with the cream cheese mixture. Ensure each wedge receives a good amount of filling.

instructions.title: Prepare the Garlic Butter Glaze
instructions.text: In another bowl, combine the melted unsalted butter, minced garlic, mayonnaise, chopped fresh parsley, beaten egg, and optional condensed milk. Whisk everything together until thoroughly combined.

instructions.title: Dip and Bake Preparation
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Carefully dip each cream cheese-filled bread wedge into the garlic butter glaze, ensuring all sides are coated. Place the bread on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown and the garlic butter is fragrant and bubbling. Serve immediately while warm.

tips: For an authentic Korean street food texture, use a soft, slightly sweet bread like a brioche bun or milk bread. If using a crusty boule, ensure it's fresh for easier cutting and absorption.
Adjust the amount of garlic to your preference; for a milder flavor, you can briefly saute the minced garlic in a tablespoon of butter before adding it to the glaze.
This bread is best served warm, straight from the oven. If you have leftovers, reheat them briefly in a toaster oven or air fryer to restore crispness.
article:
Unlock a world of irresistible flavors with Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean, a culinary sensation that has captivated food enthusiasts globally. This isn't your average garlic bread; it's a delightful fusion of sweet and savory, creamy and crisp, all wrapped up in a soft, pillowy bread. Originating from the vibrant streets of South Korea, this unique take on a classic has become a staple in Korean bakeries and a beloved treat for many. If you're ready to dive into the heart of Korean baking and discover why this particular bread is so special, read on.

## What is Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean?

At its core, Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean is a meticulously crafted bread that balances rich, contrasting flavors. Imagine a soft, often brioche-like or milk bread bun, deeply cut into wedges but not separated. These cuts are then generously filled with a sweet, tangy cream cheese mixture. The entire bun is then dunked or brushed thoroughly with a savory, garlicky butter sauce, often sweetened slightly with honey or condensed milk. Finally, it's baked until golden brown, resulting in a bread that is crispy on the outside, incredibly soft within, and bursting with a symphony of sweet cream cheese and pungent garlic butter in every bite.

Unlike traditional Italian garlic bread, which leans purely savory and often uses a baguette, the Korean version embraces a signature sweet-savory profile. The soft, enriched bread itself is a hallmark of Korean baking, providing the perfect canvas for the indulgent fillings and toppings. It’s a snack, a breakfast item, a dessert, or even a light meal – versatile and utterly delicious. The combination might sound unusual at first, but one bite of Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean will convince you of its genius.

## Ingredients You Need

To create this delectable Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean at home, you'll need a few key ingredients. Quality matters here, especially for the dairy components, to achieve that authentic taste and texture.

For the Bread:
*   6 soft bread buns (brioche buns, milk bread buns, or even dinner rolls work best)

For the Cream Cheese Filling:
*   8 oz (226g) cream cheese, softened
*   1/4 cup (50g) granulated sugar (adjust to taste)
*   1 tablespoon heavy cream or milk
*   1 teaspoon vanilla extract (optional, but enhances flavor)

For the Garlic Butter Topping:
*   1/2 cup (113g) unsalted butter, melted
*   4-5 cloves garlic, minced very finely
*   1 tablespoon honey or condensed milk (for that signature sweet note)
*   1 large egg, lightly beaten
*   1 tablespoon fresh parsley, chopped (for garnish and flavor)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   Pinch of black pepper

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Creating Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean is a fun and rewarding process. Follow these steps for perfect results:

1.  **Prepare the Bread:** Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Take your soft bread buns and make deep cuts into each bun, creating 6-8 wedges, but ensure you don't cut all the way through the bottom. The cuts should allow you to open up the bread like a flower, creating pockets for the filling.
2.  **Make the Cream Cheese Filling:** In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, granulated sugar, heavy cream (or milk), and vanilla extract (if using). Beat with a whisk or an electric mixer until the mixture is smooth, creamy, and lump-free. Transfer this filling into a piping bag (or a ziploc bag with a corner snipped off) for easier application.
3.  **Fill the Bread:** Carefully open the cuts in each bread bun and generously pipe the cream cheese filling into each wedge. Aim to fill each pocket evenly. Don't be shy with the filling, but avoid overfilling to prevent it from oozing out excessively during baking.
4.  **Prepare the Garlic Butter Topping:** In a separate bowl, whisk together the melted unsalted butter, finely minced garlic, honey (or condensed milk), the lightly beaten egg, salt, and black pepper. Stir until all ingredients are well combined.
5.  **Coat the Bread:** This is a crucial step for the signature flavor of Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean. You have two options:
    *   **Dunking Method:** Carefully hold each filled bread bun and dunk it entirely into the garlic butter mixture, ensuring all sides and crevices are thoroughly coated.
    *   **Brushing Method:** Place the filled buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Use a pastry brush to liberally brush the garlic butter mixture over the entire surface of each bun, making sure to get into all the cuts and over the cream cheese filling.
6.  **Bake:** Place the coated bread buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown, the garlic butter is bubbling, and the cream cheese filling is warm and slightly gooey.
7.  **Garnish and Serve:** Once baked, remove the Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean from the oven. Immediately sprinkle with fresh chopped parsley. Let it cool for a few minutes before serving warm.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving bakery-quality Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean at home is easier with these expert tips:

*   **Choose the Right Bread:** The foundation of this dish is a soft, slightly sweet bread. Milk bread buns (also known as Hokkaido milk bread) or brioche buns are ideal choices. Their tender crumb and slight sweetness perfectly complement the fillings. Avoid crusty breads like baguettes.
*   **Soften Cream Cheese Properly:** Ensure your cream cheese is at room temperature before mixing. This prevents lumps and ensures a smooth, uniform filling.
*   **Don't Skimp on Garlic Butter:** The garlic butter is what gives this bread its iconic flavor and glossy finish. Be generous when coating the bread. The egg in the mixture helps create a beautiful golden crust.
*   **Fine Mince Garlic:** For the best flavor distribution without large chunks of raw garlic, mince your garlic cloves very finely or use a garlic press.
*   **Watch the Baking Time:** Ovens vary, so keep an eye on your bread. You want it golden brown and fragrant, but not burnt. The cream cheese should be heated through and slightly molten.
*   **Serve Warm:** Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean is best enjoyed warm, fresh out of the oven, when the bread is soft, the filling is gooey, and the garlic butter is aromatic.

## Serving Suggestions

Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways:

*   **As a Standalone Snack:** It's rich enough to be a satisfying snack on its own, perfect for an afternoon treat.
*   **Breakfast or Brunch:** Pair it with a cup of coffee, tea, or a glass of milk for a delightful and indulgent breakfast or brunch item.
*   **Appetizer:** Cut into smaller pieces, it makes an excellent appetizer for gatherings, offering a unique twist on classic party foods.
*   **Dessert:** Given its sweet elements, it can also serve as a unique, savory-sweet dessert after a light meal.
*   **Side Dish:** While rich, it can complement certain light soups or salads, adding a touch of indulgence to your meal.

## Variations and Substitutions

Feel free to experiment with your Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean to suit your taste:

*   **Different Breads:** While milk bread is ideal, you can try challah rolls, Hawaiian sweet rolls, or even homemade soft dinner rolls.
*   **Sweetness Levels:** Adjust the amount of sugar in the cream cheese filling and honey/condensed milk in the garlic butter to your preference. Some prefer it sweeter, others less so.
*   **Herbs:** While parsley is traditional, you can add other fresh herbs to the garlic butter, such as chives or a hint of dried oregano, for a different aromatic profile.
*   **Spicy Kick:** A pinch of red pepper flakes in the garlic butter can add a subtle heat for those who enjoy a bit of spice.
*   **Cheese Additions:** For an extra cheesy experience, you could mix a small amount of shredded mozzarella or parmesan into the cream cheese filling, or sprinkle some on top before baking.
*   **Vegan Options:** With the rise of plant-based ingredients, you can find excellent vegan cream cheese and butter alternatives to make a delicious vegan version of this treat.

Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean is more than just a recipe; it's an experience. It embodies the innovative spirit of Korean baking, blending familiar flavors into something entirely new and utterly addictive. Whether you're a seasoned baker or a curious food lover, trying your hand at this delightful bread will be a rewarding culinary adventure. Enjoy the sweet, savory, and garlicky perfection!
